If these are supplied by untrusted sources, there could be risk when calling the contract, but **this is outside the scope of this factory code** itself. The factory merely facilitates interaction; it does not process or validate inputs beyond passing them along.\n- No assumptions about the contract's internal logic are necessary here; the interface is standard.\n\n### Consistency of scores:\n- All reports' scores are consistent with the analysis that the code is benign and standard.\n- No adjustments needed.\n\n---\n\n### Final assessment:\n\n**Malware score:**  \n**0** — No malicious payloads or behaviors detected.\n\n**Obfuscated score:**  \n**0** — Clear, standard autogenerated code.\n\n**Risk score:**  \n**0** — No security risks identified in this factory code.\n\n---\n\n### Overall conclusion:\nThe code is a typical, autogenerated ethers.js contract factory for a specific smart contract.
